title: NOC 42204 – Religion Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Religion Workers under NOC 42204 support faith-based communities by assisting with worship services, spiritual guidance, outreach programs, and social assistance initiatives. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation typically requires formal religious training or theological education depending on the denomination or faith tradition. Religion Workers contribute to spiritual well-being, community development, and social outreach across Canada.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group422 – Paraprofessional occupations in legal, social, community, and education servicesMinor Group4220 – Paraprofessional occupationsNOC Code42204

duties: Assist at public worship services and religious ceremonies
Support faith-based youth and family programs
Conduct outreach and community engagement initiatives
Organize and administer charitable programs (food banks, shelters)
Provide moral and pastoral support in hospitals, prisons, and schools
Plan and lead worship services for specific groups
employment_requirements: Completion of college, university, or other religious studies programs may be required
Theological training may be required depending on denomination
Additional requirements vary by faith tradition and institution
Religious appointment or endorsement may be necessary
Some positions may require prior missionary or ministry experience.
salary_prospects: $25,100 – $53,700 per year,depending on experience, institution type, and province.
Compensation may vary significantly depending on the size and resources of the religious organization.
labour_market_demand: Opportunities in charitable and humanitarian programs
Stable employment in established religious institutions
Faith-based organizations across Canada frequently seek trained and experienced religious workers to support diverse congregations.
pathways: Religion Workers fall under TEER 2 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
Express Entry (if eligibility criteria are met)
If you are planning to serve as a Religion Worker in Canada, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your immigration options.
